preamble: The shift from the velar stop [g] (or its fricative variant [ɣ]) to a uvular fricative like [ʁ] or [χ] involves lenition followed by backing. The articulation moves from the soft palate further down into the throat. This is a common feature of 'guttural' European soundscapes.

examples.note: In some Levantine Arabic dialects, Proto-Semitic *g (> Classical Arabic /dʒ/) backed further to [ɣ]/[ʁ] under regional phonological pressure.
The 'hard G' of the Northern Netherlands involves backing the historical velar fricative to the uvula.
Under areal influence from French or German, some dialects back the velar stop to a uvular fricative.
